fields.description:
## Dependencies (STACKED â€” review with --ref HEAD)
Builds on **ddbe** (identity entity with `mappings: [{provider, external_id}]`),
committed on this branch, not yet on origin/main. Review with `--ref HEAD`.

## Context
Resolve outbound Jira assignee/reporter by the provider's opaque **accountId**,
looked up through the identity entity's `{provider, external_id}` mappings, with
graceful degradation. Mappings key on the opaque id, NEVER email.

## Architectural constraint (drives every placement below)
`acli.py` is the reconciler ENGINE's Jira client: it imports only within the
`rebar_reconciler` package (its own `acli_*`/`adapters`/`adf` submodules) and must NOT
reach up into rebar's CORE library (`rebar._commands.*`) â€” that layering keeps the
engine separable from rebar core and avoids a circular import (rebar core â†’ engine â†’
rebar core). (Its docstring's "stdlib only" line is stale re: intra-package imports,
but the core-library boundary holds.) Therefore ALL identity/mapping resolution lives
in the reconciler FLOW layer (`outbound_differ.py`, `dispatch_one.py`, which already
depend on rebar core); `acli.py`/`acli_rest.py` receive only a bool flag + resolved
accountId strings.

## What
### 1. Provider-neutral seam (`src/rebar/_commands/identity.py`)
- `resolve_mapping(provider, external_id, *, repo_root=None) -> str | None` â€” id of the
  identity whose `mappings` contains `{"provider":provider,"external_id":external_id}`
  (exact match on the opaque id), else None. (AC1 seam; 2f13 also consumes it.) Never raises.
- `jira_account_id(local_assignee, *, repo_root=None) -> str | None` â€” resolve a LOCAL
  assignee string to a Jira accountId: an identity whose ticket id == `local_assignee`
  OR whose `email` == it (case-insensitive); return its `{provider:"jira"}`
  `external_id`, else None. Never raises.

### 2. Assignee accountId fast-path (resolve in the DIFFER, skip flag through to acli)
- `_assignee_resolver` (`outbound_differ.py`, flow layer, may import rebar) tries
  `identity.jira_account_id(assignee, repo_root=config.repo_root())` FIRST. On a hit it
  returns the accountId; the differ writes that accountId into `changed['assignee']`
  AND sets a sentinel `changed['_assignee_is_account_id'] = True`. (`OutboundMutation`
  is a frozen dataclass â€” the sentinel rides INSIDE the `fields`/`changed` dict, not a
  new dataclass field.)
- `dispatch_one.update_one` POPS `_assignee_is_account_id` off `fields` (a small
  `_update_one_apply_*`-style step) and passes `assignee_is_account_id=<bool>` as a
  kwarg into `client.update_issue`.
- `AcliClient.update_issue` (stdlib-only) gains `assignee_is_account_id: bool = False`;
  it POPS this kwarg before delegating to the module-level `update_issue(**kwargs)` (so
  it is never forwarded to the ACLI subprocess as `--assignee_is_account_id`), and when
  True it submits `assignee` DIRECTLY (skips `validate_assignee_exists`). No rebar
  import enters acli.py â€” only a bool.
- Degradation UNCHANGED: no mapping â†’ the existing `validate_assignee_exists`
  string-match â†’ `AssigneeNotFoundError` â†’ soft-fail â†’ unassigned/project-default.
  264f never hard-fails on an unresolvable assignee.

### 3. `/user/search` bootstrap (best-effort, TRANSIENT, flow layer)
In `_assignee_resolver`'s fallback (flow layer), when `jira_account_id` misses but the
identity has an `email`, best-effort call the client's `/rest/api/3/user/search?query=
<email>` (a helper in `acli_rest.py` that takes an email and returns an accountId or
None). The v3 endpoint returns a JSON LIST of user objects each with `accountId` +
`emailAddress`; extract the `accountId` of the entry whose `emailAddress` matches the
email EXACTLY (case-insensitive). Since Jira substring-matches, ZERO or â‰Ą2 exact
matches â†’ NO result (do not guess). The resolved accountId is used for THIS run only
(NOT written back to `mappings`; persistence out of scope). Any miss/ambiguity/hidden
email degrades to the string-match/unassigned path WITHOUT failing.

### 4. Reporter by accountId â€” dedicated REST sub-call, resolved in the flow layer
- `dispatch_one.update_one` gains `_update_one_apply_reporter(fields, issue_key,
  client)`, called BEFORE `_update_one_filter_fields` (mirroring
  `_update_one_apply_parent` at line 518â†’493): it POPS `reporter` off `fields`,
  resolves the local reporter string to an accountId via
  `identity.jira_account_id(reporter, repo_root=config.repo_root())`, and (when
  resolved) calls `client.set_reporter(issue_key, account_id)`. Because reporter is
  popped BEFORE the allowlist filter, it is NOT added to `_OUTBOUND_BATCH_ALLOWLIST`
  and never reaches the scalar `client.update_issue(**fields)`. `reporter` IS added to
  `outbound_fields.py` so the differ emits it into `changed`.
- `acli_rest.set_reporter(jira_key, account_id)` uses `_direct_rest_put_raw`
  (NOT `_direct_rest_put`, which wraps as `{"value": ...}` for the properties API):
  `_direct_rest_put_raw(f"/rest/api/3/issue/{jira_key}", {"fields": {"reporter":
  {"accountId": account_id}}})`.
- Failure is HTTP-ONLY (no subprocess): catch `urllib.error.HTTPError` (4xx =
  Modify-Reporter not granted) around ONLY the `set_reporter` call, record
  `alert_store.append(...)` kind `outbound-reporter-not-permitted` (fields `jira_key`,
  `field="reporter"`, `reason`), and let `update_one` continue (soft degradation) so
  OTHER fields still apply and the sync does not hard-fail.

## Scope
In: the `resolve_mapping`/`jira_account_id` seam, the assignee accountId fast-path
(differ â†’ sentinel â†’ acli bool), the transient `/user/search` bootstrap, and the
reporter REST sub-call with HTTP-only isolated degradation. Out: inbound ghost minting
(2f13), enforcement (3183), persisting bootstrapped accountIds. Tests use a fixture
identity + a stubbed client (no live Jira).

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] `resolve_mapping(provider, external_id)` returns the mapped identity id (keyed on
  the opaque id, not email) and None when absent; `jira_account_id` returns the jira
  accountId for a matching local assignee and None otherwise (test both).
- [ ] With a fixture identity mapping a local assignee to a stubbed accountId, the
  outbound mutation carries that accountId as the assignee and the stubbed client's
  `validate_assignee_exists`/assignable-search is NOT called â€” asserted by a spy whose
  call-count is 0 (measurable "no Jira search needed").
- [ ] An assignee with no identity mapping and a stubbed assignable-search miss
  degrades to unassigned/project-default WITHOUT raising or failing the sync (test).
- [ ] `/user/search` bootstrap: an identity with an email but no jira mapping resolves
  via a stubbed `/user/search` exact-email match to that accountId (transiently); a
  stubbed miss or â‰Ą2-match degrades without failing (test both).
- [ ] Reporter is applied via `set_reporter` (REST) with the resolved accountId when
  the stub succeeds; when the stubbed `set_reporter` raises an HTTP 4xx, `update_one`
  records an `alert_store` entry of kind `outbound-reporter-not-permitted`, still
  applies other fields, and does not fail the sync (test asserts the alert, other
  fields applied, overall success).

## file_impact
- `src/rebar/_commands/identity.py` (resolve_mapping + jira_account_id seam)
- `src/rebar/_engine/rebar_reconciler/outbound_differ.py` (_assignee_resolver: identity fast-path + /user/search bootstrap + _assignee_is_account_id sentinel)
- `src/rebar/_engine/rebar_reconciler/dispatch_one.py` (pop _assignee_is_account_id â†’ assignee_is_account_id kwarg; _update_one_apply_reporter pop+resolve+set_reporter before the filter)
- `src/rebar/_engine/rebar_reconciler/acli.py` (update_issue: assignee_is_account_id bool param, popped before delegation, skips validate_assignee_exists)
- `src/rebar/_engine/rebar_reconciler/acli_rest.py` (/user/search emailâ†’accountId helper + set_reporter via _direct_rest_put_raw)
- `src/rebar/_engine/rebar_reconciler/outbound_fields.py` (emit reporter into changed)
